note_on_counts: File/dir/LOC counts are no longer hand-maintained here. See docs/catalog/MODULE_REGISTRY.json (metrics block) generated by scripts/generate_catalog.py to keep numbers verifiable. As of P-FIX-5 (2026-07-22), `generate_catalog.py --check` recomputes metrics and renders Markdown in memory and diffs both against what is on disk - see docs/reference or tests/integration/test_catalog_drift_detection.py for the negative tests that prove it.

validation.python_tests: Verify by running `python -m pytest -q` - do not read a number from this file, it goes stale every tranche (that staleness was itself a 2026-07-22 review finding).
validation.catalog_verify: PASS (python scripts/generate_catalog.py --check) - as of P-FIX-5 this recomputes metrics and Markdown and fails on drift; verified with negative tests.

cvf_enforcement.2026-07-22_correction: A prior version of this file said 'golden_vertical: Operational Event -> confirm' and listed 8 controls as enforced. An independent review (docs/decisions/EA_INDEPENDENT_REVIEW_2026-07-22_CODEX.md) found that label was overbroad. A corrective tranche (P-FIX-0 through P-FIX-5, see docs/implementation/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md) fixed the Critical/High findings. See docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md for the current, evidence-based status of each control - this file only summarizes it and can drift, so treat CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md as authoritative.
cvf_enforcement.2026-07-22_pfix6_correction: After P-FIX-5 claimed the P-FIX tranche CLOSED, a SECOND independent review rejected that claim: POST /shifts/{shift_id}/close still called ledger.close_shift() directly from the router with no identity/permission/audit at all (probe: create=200, anonymous_close=200, status=CLOSED, audit_count=0). Because ShiftService.freeze only checks shift.status == ShiftStatus.CLOSED, that anonymous close could silently satisfy freeze's shift_closed prerequisite - a governance bypass inside the very tranche that claimed to have fixed governance bypasses. P-FIX-6 added shift.close as a real governed action (ShiftService.close, same identity/permission/audit shape as freeze) and re-synced every front-door file. Status is now P-FIX CLOSED_BOUNDED, not 'all findings fixed'.
fixed_by_pfix_tranche: freeze (P-FIX-1: now load-bearing, cross-record, both backends)
audit (P-FIX-2: now atomic with mutation, both backends)
evidence (P-FIX-3: now persisted through SqlLedger for events and tasks)
approval (P-FIX-3: now rejects fabricated/inflated approver claims via known-principals.yaml - interim, not full auth)
shift.close (P-FIX-6: now a governed action - identity/permission/audit via ShiftService.close, same shape as freeze; previously bypassed governance entirely)
cvf_enforcement.2026-07-22_p2b_correction: P2-B (2026-07-22, commit cd36b27) changed dependencies.py::get_principal to require a verified JWT bearer token instead of trusting X-User-Id/X-User-Role headers. This did NOT touch known-principals.yaml or approval quorum - High Finding #4 (approval-fabrication) remains open. See p2b_authentication block below and docs/decisions/ADR_2026-07-22_P2B_JWT_AUTHENTICATION.md.
cvf_enforcement.2026-07-22_p2b_corrective_intake: INTAKE of a corrective tranche (P2B-AUTHENTICATION-REPAIR) downgraded the original P2-B claim: cd36b27 was built, reviewed, and closed with NO recorded DESIGN, SPEC, or operator-approved WORK_ORDER (control-chain bypass per AGENTS.md's seven-step chain). cd36b27 is NOT reverted (it remains as historical evidence).
cvf_enforcement.2026-07-23_p2b_corrective_freeze: The corrective tranche completed BUILD (2c397f7), repaired all 8 findings from independent REVIEW #1 (10e57e1), received REVIEW_PASS from independent REVIEW #2, then repaired a regional-endpoint finding from the first live attempt (bf7c328). The rerun bound the real JWT identity gate to a real Alibaba qwen3.7-max call and returned HTTP 200 with the expected token. Sanitized receipt: docs/decisions/P2B_IDENTITY_LIVE_EVIDENCE_RECEIPT.md. Disposition: FREEZE; identity is load-bearing and governance-approved within the stated boundary. Approval/known-principals High Finding #4 remains open.

known_remaining_defects.note: High Finding #4 (approval-fabrication) is NOT fixed - P2-B is identity only, and explicitly did not touch known-principals.yaml or approval. Do not write 'all High findings fixed' anywhere.
items: data minimization is still advisory only
data_scope/cost/termination have no runtime caller yet
known-principals is a registry check, not real authentication, and P2-B did not reconcile it with the new users table
P2-B has no refresh token/revocation and no real admin user-provisioning flow (scripts/seed_dev_users.py is dev/test only)

cvf_enforcement.tranche_and_commit_count: 5 implementation tranches P-FIX-1 through P-FIX-5, plus the preparatory P-FIX-0 tranche; 6 commits before P-FIX-6. After P-FIX-6's commit, the total is 7 P-FIX commits (counting P-FIX-0).

p2a_customer_request.2026-07-22_tranche: P2-A-CUSTOMER-REQUEST: replicated the CVF chain (same shape as TaskService/ShiftService) to the customer_request domain. CustomerRequest model + CustomerRequestStatus lifecycle (domain/lifecycle.py), customer_requests table mapped in tables.py (nullable shift_id FK, second FK to messages.message_id, status CHECK matching migration 002 exactly - verified two-directionally by the schema-parity suite), add/get/put_customer_request on the Ledger Protocol + InMemoryLedger + SqlLedger, CustomerRequestService (create: identity -> permission -> domain_lock -> persist(frozen-shift check only when shift_id given) -> audit; transition: identity -> permission -> lifecycle guard -> persist -> audit), router at POST /customer-requests + POST /customer-requests/{id}/transition, registered in main.py.
p2a_customer_request.scope_statement: customer_request is DONE. incidents and handovers - the other two domains named under P2-A in EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md - are NOT done: neither has a migration table yet (a new migration would be required first), so implementing them was explicitly out of scope for this tranche. Do not round this up to 'P2-A closed'.
p2a_customer_request.known_limits: customer_requests has no risk_class/evidence/version column in the migration (unlike tasks/operational_events), so create_customer_request does not wire evidence/approval gates - this is a deliberate match to the simpler schema, not a missing feature. Repo-wide limit at the time this tranche was written: identity was header-based (not authenticated) - superseded 2026-07-22 by P2-B, see p2b_authentication block below. PostgreSQL round-trip has never been run live (no Docker in this environment).
p2a_customer_request.tests_added: tests/cvf/test_customer_request_vertical.py (18 tests: service-level + HTTP-level create, permission 401/403, domain-lock-allowed create, valid/invalid lifecycle transitions including the WAITING-cannot-skip-to-CLOSED design choice, CLOSED terminal, atomic audit-rollback on both backends, create with/without shift_id, create rejected when parent shift is FROZEN).

p2b_authentication.governance_disposition: FREEZE (2026-07-23). cd36b27 remains the historical unauthorized build candidate and was never rewritten; the corrective tranche supplied the missing DESIGN/SPEC/approved WORK_ORDER, BUILD/REPAIR, independent REVIEW_PASS, and live Alibaba governance evidence PASS. Identity is load-bearing and governance-approved within this bounded P2-B claim. Approval/known-principals High Finding #4 remains open.
p2b_authentication.2026-07-22_tranche: P2-B: replaced header-trusting identity with real authentication. New users table (database/migrations/003_users.sql: text primary key, unique username, bcrypt password_hash, role CHECK matching cvf_runtime.identity.KNOWN_ROLES, is_active) mapped in tables.py + add_user/get_user_by_username on the Ledger Protocol + InMemoryLedger + SqlLedger. New workspace_api/auth/ module: passwords.py (bcrypt hash/verify), tokens.py (create_access_token/decode_access_token, JWT HS256 signed with required JWT_SECRET_KEY env var, no default so the app fails closed at startup), router.py (POST /auth/login). dependencies.py::get_principal rewritten to require and verify a JWT bearer token instead of X-User-Id/X-User-Role headers - every router's `Depends(get_principal)` signature is unchanged.
p2b_authentication.scope_statement: identity is now load-bearing for all five governed domains (Event/Correction/Task/Shift/CustomerRequest), since they all resolve Principal through the same get_principal dependency. Explicitly OUT of scope, not silently dropped: refresh tokens/revocation, self-service registration, password reset, login rate-limiting, and reconciling known-principals.yaml (the separate approver registry used by approval quorum) with the new users table - High Finding #4 (approval-fabrication) is UNCHANGED by this tranche.
p2b_authentication.known_limits: User provisioning is scripts/seed_dev_users.py only (dev/test, reuses known-principals.yaml's ids for fixture legibility - the two registries remain independent stores) - no real admin-provisioning flow exists yet. Access tokens have a fixed TTL (JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_MINUTES, default 60) with no refresh or early revocation.
p2b_authentication.tests_added: tests/cvf/test_auth_tokens.py (8 tests: round-trip, tampered signature, expiry, wrong signing key, malformed token, alg=none, unknown role claim, TTL sourced from settings), tests/cvf/test_auth_login.py (5 tests: valid login, wrong password, unknown username with the same response as wrong password, inactive user, unknown-username still runs password verification), tests/cvf/test_shift_close_governance.py::test_old_header_impersonation_no_longer_grants_any_identity (regression proof: claiming authorized_executive via the old headers alone, no bearer token, is still 401), tests/integration/test_schema_parity_users.py (role CHECK values compared two-directionally against KNOWN_ROLES).
p2b_authentication.2026-07-22_independent_review: A fresh agent (no memory of this implementation) independently probed the running code: old-header impersonation, alg=none, wrong signing key, expired token, forged unknown-role claim, and a real login round-trip - no bypass found in any of those vectors; jwt_secret_key confirmed to have no default; every governed router endpoint confirmed to still depend on get_principal unchanged; known-principals.yaml/approval.py confirmed untouched. It found ONE real gap: the login endpoint had a timing side-channel - verify_password (bcrypt) was skipped entirely for an unknown username (Python `or` short-circuit), making that response ~18x faster than a wrong-password response despite an identical response body, enumerating valid usernames by timing alone. Fixed in this same tranche before commit: verify_password now always runs, against a precomputed DUMMY_PASSWORD_HASH when no user is found (workspace_api/auth/passwords.py).

cvf_core_pin.scope_note: This block records a GOVERNANCE/TOOLING tranche, not a roadmap feature. It changes no phase status, no module status, and no roadmap item. The `status` field at the top of this file is deliberately unchanged by it.
cvf_core_pin.problem: The CVF workspace doctor was failing 23/24 with 'CVF public core matches origin/main -> BEHIND_PUBLIC_REMOTE': the hidden sibling core sat at c1076dc while public origin/main had moved to 6ce1cf0. AGENTS.md's First-Request Protocol requires a passing doctor before material work, so every later tranche would have started from a failing gate.
cvf_core_pin.what_changed: The hidden sibling CVF core was reconciled to public origin/main using the framework's own sanctioned reconciler (.Controlled-Vibe-Framework-CVF/scripts/update_cvf_workspace_public_core.ps1, invoked with -WorkspaceRoot only; -UpdateProjectManifests, -AllowPendingCoreBackup and -OverlaySourcePath were prohibited and unused), and .cvf/manifest.json's cvfCoreCommit was re-pinned to the full 40-character hash. The in-repository BUILD changed set was exactly one file, one line.

commits.authorization_artifacts: 76e7360 - ADR + SPEC + WORK_ORDER only, zero implementation files, proving DESIGN/SPEC/WORK_ORDER preceded BUILD in the commit graph itself.
commits.build_review_pass: da9a122 - .cvf/manifest.json only, 1 insertion / 1 deletion, after independent REVIEW_PASS.
commits.freeze_authorization_addendum: 18d67d3 - SPEC section 9 + WORK_ORDER section 13, authorizing the continuity work that had been deliberately deferred until the P2-B FREEZE batch was committed.
commits.p2b_freeze_separate: 4e15ea4 - the P2-B FREEZE batch, committed separately. da9a122 and 4e15ea4 share no path, which is the direct evidence the two tranches were never batched into one commit.

cvf_core_pin.verified_end_state: Hidden core HEAD, hidden core origin/main, and .cvf/manifest.json cvfCoreCommit are all 6ce1cf00c31a7f825d4c3fa3e66e8a3509e4a4b2; hidden core worktree clean; workspace doctor RESULT: PASS (24/24 checks passed) with FRESH_CLONE_CONTINUITY_PASS.
cvf_core_pin.upstream_delta: A single commit, 6ce1cf0 'docs: reconcile seven-step loop and provider claims', touching four CVF core documentation files (ARCHITECTURE.md, PROVIDERS.md, README.md, docs/reference/CVF_PROVIDER_LANE_READINESS_MATRIX.md). No CVF core script, governance toolkit template, or workspace kit file changed. Those four upstream documents were accepted as the public core's own state and were NOT audited for content correctness by this project.
cvf_core_pin.claim_boundary: This tranche establishes only that the hidden core matches public origin/main at 6ce1cf0, that the manifest pin matches it, and that local enforcement artifacts pass 24/24. It is NOT live governance evidence of AI/agent behavior - no provider call was made or required, because no AI-governance claim is asserted. It does not alter the P2-B FREEZE disposition or its receipt, does not close High Finding #4, does not advance any roadmap item, and does not make any CVF control newly load-bearing. cd36b27 remains the original unauthorized build candidate, retained unrewritten.
cvf_core_pin.open_continuity_drift: CONTRIBUTING.md:21 rules that the next unchecked roadmap item is taken in order, and docs/implementation/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md:207 shows that item is P1-B (extract domain models into operations-domain), but the recorded lane list offered only P2-A remaining, known-principals-to-users, and P2-C, with P1-B absent. RESOLVED by the operator at INTAKE on 2026-07-23: the operator confirmed the order P1-B, then known-principals.yaml-to-users reconciliation, then P2-A incidents/handovers, then P2-C, and opened lane 1 (P1-B) only. No agent chose or ranked a lane and the roadmap was not edited to make the surfaces agree. See p1b_operations_domain_extraction below.

p1b_operations_domain_extraction.scope_note: This block records a roadmap-item closure (Phase 1 P1-B). It changes no other module's status. operations-domain moves stub -> partial ONLY.
p1b_operations_domain_extraction.what_changed: The 12 operational types (DataState, RiskClass, ShiftStatus, TaskStatus, CustomerRequestStatus, EvidenceRef, Shift, Message, OperationalEvent, Correction, Task, CustomerRequest) and the 3 lifecycle guards (assert_transition, assert_task_transition, assert_customer_request_transition) now have a SINGLE canonical definition in packages/operations-domain/src/operations_domain/ (models.py, lifecycle.py), moved verbatim out of workspace-api. workspace_api.domain.models and workspace_api.domain.lifecycle are now compatibility shims that re-export those same objects with proven OBJECT IDENTITY (`is`, not `==`, asserted per module pair), not redefinitions. All moved-type imports were repointed to operations_domain (32 import lines changed); the documented User imports, the SqlLedger shim-namespace imports, and the shim-identity test's imports intentionally remain. The operations-domain package imports only the standard library and pydantic (a dependency sink): it never imports workspace_api, operations_ledger, cvf_runtime, fastapi or sqlalchemy, enforced by a repository-scoped import-isolation subprocess.
p1b_operations_domain_extraction.user_not_moved: User did NOT move. It mirrors database/migrations/003_users.sql and belongs to the authentication boundary, so its canonical home stays workspace_api.domain.models. workspace_api.domain.models is therefore BOTH a compatibility shim (for the 12 operational types) AND the canonical home of User. The eventual relocation of User is owned by the known-principals.yaml <-> users reconciliation tranche (High Finding #4), not this one.
p1b_operations_domain_extraction.ledger_seam_untouched: packages/operations-ledger/** has a ZERO-LINE diff. The SqlLedger(models=...) injection seam and the Ledger Protocol were deliberately NOT refactored: operations_ledger._rows.build_user needs models.User, so the injected namespace (workspace_api.domain.models) must keep exposing both the re-exported operational types and User. Tightening the Protocol to import operations_domain directly is recorded as a later-tranche follow-up, unblocked but not authorized by P1-B.
p1b_operations_domain_extraction.module_status: operations-domain: stub -> partial. NOT enforced. The canonical models/lifecycle exist for shifts, messages, events, tasks, customer requests and corrections, but incidents, handovers, reports, approvals and audit still have NO operational model or runtime in the package, and the per-domain blueprint subdirectories are still README-only (models live together in models.py). Adding a missing domain, or splitting models.py into per-domain submodules, is a separate tranche each. See docs/catalog/MODULE_REGISTRY.json operations-domain entry (authoritative).

Moved the two catalog paths from C4 into C3 and corrected the path-count expectation, after a catalog-gate conflict was found during BUILD.
commits.build_review_pass: f68cf634f67984d64562dd8bfd0962ef01eb91fe (C3) - the 42-path BUILD, after independent REVIEW_PASS on AC-01..AC-18.

p1b_operations_domain_extraction.catalog_gate_conflict: During BUILD, generate_catalog.py --check (hardened by P-FIX-5 to recompute metrics from source) failed because C3 necessarily changed LOC/file metrics, and validate_repository.py calls that same check - so with docs/catalog/** prohibited in C3, AC-12 (zero failures) and AC-13 (validators PASS) were jointly unsatisfiable. The IMPLEMENTATION_WORKER correctly STOPPED (did not regenerate a prohibited path, did not weaken the drift test) and reported. The authorization was amended through DESIGN->SPEC->WORK_ORDER (C2b) to move the two catalog files into C3 before BUILD resumed - the control chain catching an authorization defect rather than absorbing it silently into a BUILD commit.
p1b_operations_domain_extraction.review_evidence: Independent REVIEW_PASS on AC-01..AC-18. Targeted three new test files: 71 passed. tests/integration + tests/cvf: 210 passed. Full suite: 292 passed (221 baseline + 71 new), 0 failed, 0 errors. validate_repository.py PASS; generate_catalog.py --check PASS; check_session_state.py PASS; check_file_size.py PASS; workspace doctor 24/24. git diff --check clean. packages/operations-ledger/ zero-line diff. AC-18 rollback rehearsal ran in a temporary worktree outside the primary workspace: after reverting C3 the C3 paths matched C3_PARENT ab75abb, C1/C2/C2b remained intact, the suite returned to the 221 passed baseline, and the temporary worktree was cleaned up (PASS).
p1b_operations_domain_extraction.claim_boundary: P1-B extracted class/function definitions and repointed imports with byte-identical behavior (Pydantic dump/validate/JSON-schema, enum values, lifecycle transitions and the OpenAPI document all unchanged, compared as canonical bytes). It asserts NO AI/agent-governance claim, so no provider call was made or required. It does NOT: change any API contract, response schema, database schema, migration, lifecycle semantics, enum value, or CVF gate behavior; touch authentication, approval, or known-principals.yaml; make operations-domain enforced; advance the Phase 1 exit gate; or claim a PostgreSQL live round-trip. cd36b27 remains untouched. High Finding #4 remains OPEN.

notes: Zalo and WhatsApp are mock/conformance skeletons until official credentials and API approval are available.
Production hardening remains gated by Phase 5 acceptance evidence.

Persistence: SqlLedger (SQLite verified end-to-end incl. FK/CHECK/evidence/atomic-rollback; PostgreSQL schema defect fixed in P-FIX-4 but never run live - no Docker in this environment) plus InMemoryLedger for tests/offline. See operations-ledger entry in the module catalog.

Do not treat this file as current without cross-checking docs/catalog/MODULE_CATALOG.md and SESSION/ACTIVE_SESSION_STATE.json - front-door drift was itself a finding of the 2026-07-22 review, and recurred (P-FIX-6) even inside the tranche meant to fix it.
PostgreSQL has NEVER been verified live in this environment (no Docker daemon). Do not claim PostgreSQL round-trip verified anywhere; it remains a pre-ship gate.
P2-A (customer_request) is done as of 2026-07-22; P2-A (incidents, handovers) remains open and requires a new migration first.

P2-B real authentication reached FREEZE on 2026-07-23 through corrective tranche P2B-AUTHENTICATION-REPAIR: approved WORK_ORDER before BUILD, repair of review findings, independent REVIEW_PASS, and a real identity-bound Alibaba response (HTTP 200). cd36b27 remains historical evidence and was not rewritten. See docs/decisions/P2B_IDENTITY_LIVE_EVIDENCE_RECEIPT.md and SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_2026-07-23_P2B_AUTHENTICATION_REPAIR_FREEZE.md - that handoff is retained history and is no longer the active handoff.

CVF-CORE-PIN-2026-07-23 (governance/tooling, not a roadmap feature) reached FREEZE/CLOSED_BOUNDED on 2026-07-23: hidden CVF core and manifest pin moved from c1076dc to 6ce1cf0, restoring workspace doctor 24/24. See the cvf_core_pin block above and SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_2026-07-23_CVF_CORE_PIN_FREEZE.md. It changed no roadmap item and is not AI-governance evidence.

P1B-OPERATIONS-DOMAIN-EXTRACTION (roadmap item P1-B) reached FREEZE/CLOSED_BOUNDED on 2026-07-23: the operational models and lifecycle guards moved to the operations-domain package (single canonical definition), workspace_api.domain files became identity-proven compatibility shims, User stayed on the auth boundary, and operations-domain went stub -> partial (NOT enforced). See the p1b_operations_domain_extraction block above and SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_2026-07-23_P1B_OPERATIONS_DOMAIN_EXTRACTION.md (now the active handoff). Ticking P1-B does NOT close the Phase 1 exit gate: the PostgreSQL live round-trip has still never run and remains a pre-ship gate.

Next tranche is lane 2 of the operator's four-lane order: the known-principals.yaml <-> authenticated users reconciliation (High Finding #4), starting at a fresh INTAKE. High Finding #4 remains OPEN - it was NOT touched by P1-B. Do not claim 'all findings fixed'.
